text:
The Flesh Collector's Mansion: Part 1 (の①, Jintai Shūshūka no Yakata (Ichi)) is the 67 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Kurapika arrives at the Nostrade's mansion and says at the gate that the Sengi Guild referred him. After Kurapika reaches the mansion, a butler greets him and leads him inside. As Kurapika waits in a room with several others, he remembers returning to the Sengi Guild and speaking to the agent again. She said that she was surprised he had learned Nen in only six months, easily remembering Kurapika since only one person had found the agency since him.

The agent asked him what kind of employer he wanted, and Kurapika replied that it had to be someone with strong connections to the Yorknew auction. She warned him that those types of clients had strict standards and were unlikely to hire people with no experience, but she found three offers involving personal protection that were willing to hire after an interview: a handgun buff, an antiques collector, and a flesh collector.

As Kurapika's mind returns to the present, the butler welcomes the group to the contract briefing and directs their attention to a large screen. With two women beside him, a man appears on the screen and says that they won't ask them if they are licensed, only requiring that the applicants can get what they want. He says that the auction is in one month and that each of them needs to bring back one item from the list the butler will give them. The butler hands out the data cards, and Kurapika immediately sees that eyes from the Kurta Clan are on the list, which specifies that they must be scarlet and that a set complete with the head would be preferred.

One of the other applicants says that Kurapika looks pale and tells him that all the items are legal to own, adding that they will have to acquire even worse things once they are hired. He starts to talk about one of his clients who wanted a newborn, but Kurapika tells him to be quiet and not to talk to him. The man on screen tells the applicants that once they're hired, they will be in charge of acquisitions and security. After he wishes them good luck, Kurapika tells himself that he will first earn their trust, confident that the man isn't even the actual client. He plans to get as close as he can so that he can learn their secrets, saying that collectors desire both rare items and people to whom they can show off their collection. Finally, he guesses that the various flesh collectors have ties with one another, vowing to arrest every single one of them.

The applicants go to leave the room, but find that the door is locked. As the man on screen says that they need to be tough enough to make it out alive, several sword blades pierce the door and force the applicants to retreat back. Several figures shrouded in black hoods burst into the room, with two in the back pulling out handguns and firing toward the group. The applicants hide behind cover, with one of them using the butler as a human shield. Kurapika stands ready and seems to wield something in his right hand.
